reason: misdiagnosis. The original junction-below-gate placement (y=34 between gate y=32 and step y=36) is correct per IEC 60848: feedback bypasses the target's incoming gate (gate condition only applies to forward arm). Putting feedback above the gate would force gate condition onto feedback, changing semantics. User pointed out the inconsistency 2026-05-05.
corrected_understanding: Feedback rejoin position: (a) single forward + feedback (track gate target): join AFTER track gate (between gate and step) — current behavior CORRECT. (b) multi-forward + feedback (OR-converge target): feedback enters converge bar as a 3rd input arm — different mechanism. Don't conflate.

symptom: two right-side bypass lanes coexist at x=49.93 (step 2 → converge bar) and x=48.93 (step 11 → converge bar) — separated by 1u
root_cause_detail: two bypass routes use lane_slots = 0 and 1 respectively because they are different lane_keys (transition T_step2_bypass vs transition T_step11_bypass). When both go right-side around an obstacle, they could share same lane.
fix_proposal: defer until R-7 (right-side bus) lands; right bus already plans single shared column, will subsume both.
